recipes.title: Moussaka
recipes.description: Layered Greek casserole of eggplant, spiced lamb mince, and creamy béchamel baked until golden.
steps: Slice eggplants 1cm thick, salt and rest 30 minutes, then pat dry and brush with olive oil.
Roast eggplant slices at 200°C until softened and lightly browned, about 20 minutes.
Sauté chopped onion and garlic in olive oil, add lamb and brown, then deglaze with red wine.
Stir in passata, cinnamon, salt and simmer 25 minutes until thick.
Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, gradually add warm milk, simmer until thick.
Off heat, whisk eggs and grated kefalotyri into béchamel.
Layer eggplant, meat sauce, eggplant in a baking dish, then pour béchamel on top.
Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes until deeply golden, then rest 20 minutes before slicing.

recipes.title: Souvlaki
recipes.description: Marinated pork or chicken skewers grilled over charcoal, served with pita and tzatziki.
steps: Cut pork into 3cm cubes and place in a bowl.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, crushed garlic, oregano, salt and pepper.
Pour marinade over pork, toss, cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours.
Soak wooden skewers, then thread the pork tightly onto them.
Grill over hot charcoal 3-4 minutes per side until charred and just cooked through.
Warm pitas on the grill and serve souvlaki with tzatziki, tomato and onion.

recipes.title: Spanakopita
recipes.description: Crisp phyllo pie filled with spinach, feta, dill and spring onion.
steps: Wilt spinach in a dry pan, cool and squeeze out all water.
Sauté chopped spring onions in olive oil until soft.
Combine spinach, spring onions, crumbled feta, chopped dill, eggs, nutmeg and salt.
Brush a baking tray with olive oil and layer half the phyllo, brushing each sheet with oil.
Spread the filling evenly and top with remaining phyllo, brushing each sheet.
Score the top into squares and brush generously with olive oil.
Bake at 180°C for 45-50 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Cool 15 minutes before cutting along the scored lines.

recipes.title: Dolmades
recipes.description: Tender vine leaves stuffed with rice, herbs and lemon, simmered until silky.
steps: Blanch vine leaves in hot water 2 minutes, drain and pat dry.
Sauté chopped onions in olive oil until translucent.
Stir in rice, chopped herbs, salt and 100ml water; cook 5 minutes until rice is half-done.
Place a vine leaf vein-side up, add a teaspoon of filling, fold sides and roll tightly.
Line a heavy pot with leftover leaves, then pack dolmades seam-side down in tight layers.
Pour remaining olive oil, lemon juice and 400ml water over the rolls; weight with a plate.
Cover and simmer 45 minutes on low heat until rice is tender.
Cool in the pot before serving at room temperature.

recipes.title: Tzatziki
recipes.description: Cool Greek dip of strained yogurt, cucumber, garlic and olive oil.
steps: Grate the cucumber on the coarse side of a box grater.
Salt the cucumber lightly and let drain in a sieve 15 minutes, then squeeze dry.
Mince garlic to a paste with a pinch of salt.
Stir yogurt, cucumber, garlic, olive oil, vinegar and chopped dill together.
Taste, adjust salt, and chill at least 30 minutes before serving.

recipes.title: Pastitsio
recipes.description: Greek baked pasta with cinnamon-spiced meat sauce and a thick béchamel topping.
steps: Boil pasta until just shy of al dente, drain and toss with a little butter.
Sauté chopped onion and garlic, add beef and brown, then add wine to deglaze.
Stir in passata, cinnamon, cloves and salt; simmer 30 minutes until thick.
Make béchamel: melt butter, whisk in flour, slowly add milk, cook until very thick.
Off heat, whisk eggs and most of the grated cheese into the béchamel.
Layer pasta in a deep dish, top with meat sauce, then pour over the béchamel.
Sprinkle remaining cheese and bake at 180°C for 50 minutes until golden.
Rest 20 minutes before cutting into squares.

recipes.title: Avgolemono
recipes.description: Silky Greek lemon and egg soup with chicken and rice.
steps: Simmer chicken with vegetables and salt in 2.5L water for 90 minutes; skim foam.
Remove chicken, strain stock, return stock to pot.
Add rice and simmer 15 minutes until tender.
Whisk eggs in a bowl, then whisk in lemon juice.
Slowly ladle hot stock into the egg-lemon mixture, whisking constantly to temper.
Off heat, stir tempered mixture back into the pot; do not boil.
Shred chicken meat back into the soup and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Horiatiki
recipes.description: Traditional Greek village salad of tomato, cucumber, feta, olives and oregano.
steps: Cut tomatoes into wedges and place in a wide bowl.
Slice cucumber into thick half-moons and add to the bowl.
Slice red onion thinly and pepper into rings; add along with olives.
Drizzle with olive oil and vinegar, sprinkle salt and toss gently.
Lay a thick slab of feta on top, drizzle more oil and sprinkle generously with oregano.
Serve immediately with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Gyros
recipes.description: Greek wrap of seasoned spit-roasted meat in pita with tomato, onion and tzatziki.
steps: Slice pork thinly and toss with olive oil, lemon, crushed garlic, oregano, paprika and cumin.
Marinate at least 4 hours in the fridge.
Sear pork in a hot pan in batches until deeply browned and cooked through.
Warm pitas, brushed with oil, on the same pan.
Spread tzatziki on each pita, top with pork, sliced tomato and onion.
Roll tightly and serve.

recipes.title: Baklava
recipes.description: Layered phyllo pastry filled with spiced walnuts and soaked in honey syrup.
steps: Pulse walnuts with ground cinnamon until coarsely chopped.
Brush a tray with melted butter and lay 8 phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Sprinkle a layer of walnut mix, then add 2 buttered phyllo sheets; repeat until walnuts are used.
Top with 8 buttered phyllo sheets and trim edges neatly.
Score into diamonds through all layers and bake at 170°C for 50 minutes until golden.
Simmer sugar, water, honey, lemon juice and cinnamon stick 10 minutes to make syrup.
Pour cool syrup over the hot baklava and let it absorb at least 6 hours before serving.

recipes.title: Kleftiko
recipes.description: Slow-roasted lamb wrapped in parchment with potatoes, lemon and oregano.
steps: Pierce lamb all over and rub with crushed garlic, oregano, salt, lemon and olive oil.
Marinate at least 4 hours, ideally overnight.
Cut potatoes into chunks and toss with oil, oregano and salt.
Lay a large sheet of parchment on foil; pile potatoes, set lamb on top, scatter feta.
Wrap parchment then foil to make a sealed parcel.
Bake at 160°C for 4 hours until lamb is fall-apart tender.
Open parcel, increase oven to 220°C and roast 15 minutes to brown.
Serve straight from the parcel at the table.

recipes.title: Stifado
recipes.description: Greek beef stew with sweet pearl onions, red wine, vinegar and warm spices.
steps: Cut beef into large chunks and brown in olive oil; remove.
Peel pearl onions and brown whole in the same pot.
Return beef, add garlic, wine, vinegar, passata, bay, cinnamon and cloves.
Cover and simmer on lowest heat 2 hours until beef is fork tender.
Uncover the last 15 minutes to thicken the sauce.
Serve with crusty bread or orzo.

recipes.title: Loukoumades
recipes.description: Greek honey doughnuts: crisp yeasted balls drenched in honey syrup and cinnamon.
steps: Stir yeast and sugar into warm water; rest 10 minutes until foamy.
Whisk in flour and salt to form a sticky batter.
Cover and prove 90 minutes until doubled and bubbly.
Heat oil to 180°C in a deep pot.
Scoop wet batter with a spoon dipped in water; drop tablespoons into oil.
Fry, turning, until deep golden, then drain on paper towels.
Drizzle generously with warmed honey and dust with cinnamon.

recipes.title: Saganaki
recipes.description: Pan-seared Greek cheese, crisp outside and molten inside, finished with lemon.
steps: Cut cheese into 2cm thick slabs and dip briefly in cold water.
Dredge cheese in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat olive oil in a small frying pan until shimmering.
Fry cheese 1-2 minutes per side until a deep golden crust forms.
Squeeze lemon over the top, grind black pepper and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Galaktoboureko
recipes.description: Greek custard pie of semolina cream baked between crisp phyllo and soaked in syrup.
steps: Heat milk with 200g sugar; whisk in semolina and stir until thickened.
Off heat, whisk in beaten eggs, vanilla and zest until smooth.
Brush a baking tray with butter and lay half the phyllo, brushing each sheet.
Pour custard, level the surface, then top with remaining phyllo, brushing each sheet.
Tuck edges, score the top and bake at 170°C for 60 minutes until golden.
Simmer extra sugar, water and a strip of lemon peel 8 minutes for syrup; cool.
Pour cool syrup over hot pastry; rest 4 hours before slicing.

recipes.title: Fasolada
recipes.description: Greek white bean soup with carrots, celery, tomato and good olive oil.
steps: Soak beans overnight in plenty of cold water.
Drain and place in pot with fresh water; bring to boil 5 minutes, then drain again.
Return beans to pot with chopped onion, carrot, celery, bay and 2L water.
Simmer 60-90 minutes until beans are tender.
Stir in passata, olive oil and salt; simmer another 20 minutes.
Discard bay leaves and serve with extra olive oil and crusty bread.

recipes.title: Yemista
recipes.description: Greek baked tomatoes and peppers stuffed with herbed rice.
steps: Slice the tops off tomatoes and peppers; scoop out flesh and seeds, reserving tomato pulp.
Sauté chopped onions and garlic in olive oil until soft.
Add rice, chopped tomato pulp, herbs, salt and 200ml water; cook 5 minutes.
Stuff tomatoes and peppers loosely with rice mixture; replace tops.
Arrange in a baking dish with potato wedges tucked between them.
Drizzle generously with olive oil, add 200ml water to the dish and cover with foil.
Bake at 180°C for 60 minutes, uncover and bake 30 more minutes until tender.
Cool 20 minutes and serve warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Bougatsa
recipes.description: Crisp phyllo pastry filled with sweet semolina custard and dusted with cinnamon sugar.
steps: Heat milk with sugar; whisk in semolina until thick and smooth.
Off heat, whisk in eggs and vanilla; cool slightly.
Brush a baking tray with butter and layer 6 phyllo sheets, brushing each with butter.
Spread custard, fold in sides, then layer 6 more buttered phyllo sheets on top.
Score the surface and bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until deeply golden.
Dust hot pastry with icing sugar and cinnamon; serve warm.

recipes.title: Octopus Stifado
recipes.description: Slow-braised octopus with pearl onions, red wine and warm spices.
steps: Place octopus in a dry pot, cover and cook on low until it releases its liquid; simmer 20 minutes.
Remove octopus, cut into bite-size pieces; reserve some cooking liquid.
Brown peeled pearl onions in olive oil in a clean pot.
Add octopus, garlic, wine, vinegar, passata, bay and cinnamon.
Simmer covered for 60 minutes until octopus is tender and sauce thick.
Adjust seasoning and serve with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Tiropita
recipes.description: Greek phyllo pastry triangles filled with feta and ricotta, baked until shatteringly crisp.
steps: Mash feta with a fork and stir in ricotta, eggs, dill and pepper.
Cut phyllo into long strips about 8cm wide.
Brush each strip with melted butter and olive oil.
Place a teaspoon of filling at one end and fold corner over to form a triangle, continuing to fold flag-style.
Brush tops with butter and arrange on a lined tray.
Bake at 190°C for 25 minutes until golden and puffed.
Cool 5 minutes and serve warm.

recipes.title: Skordalia
recipes.description: Pungent Greek garlic dip thickened with potato or stale bread and good olive oil.
steps: Boil potatoes whole in salted water until very tender; peel while warm.
Pound garlic with salt to a smooth paste using mortar and pestle.
Mash potatoes thoroughly and combine with garlic paste.
Drizzle in olive oil while stirring vigorously to emulsify.
Stir in vinegar and lemon juice, taste and adjust salt.
Serve at room temperature with fried fish or beetroot.

recipes.title: Briam
recipes.description: Greek roasted vegetable medley of zucchini, potatoes, peppers and tomatoes with herbs.
steps: Slice all vegetables thinly and combine in a large bowl.
Add chopped garlic, olive oil, oregano, salt and chopped parsley; toss well.
Spread in a single layer in a baking dish.
Cover with foil and bake at 180°C for 45 minutes.
Uncover, stir gently and bake 30 more minutes until edges are caramelised.
Rest 15 minutes before serving warm or at room temperature.

recipes.title: Soutzoukakia
recipes.description: Greek Smyrna meatballs spiced with cumin and braised in tomato wine sauce.
steps: Soak bread in water, then squeeze dry and tear into a bowl.
Mix beef, bread, crushed garlic, cumin, egg and salt; knead 5 minutes.
Shape into oval sausage-like meatballs.
Brown in olive oil on all sides; remove.
In the same pan, add wine to deglaze, then add passata; simmer 5 minutes.
Return meatballs, cover and simmer 25 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve over rice or with crusty bread.

recipes.title: Revithia
recipes.description: Slow-baked Greek chickpea stew with onion, lemon and olive oil.
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight with a teaspoon of bicarbonate of soda.
Rinse well and place in a clay pot or heavy casserole.
Add chopped onions, garlic, bay, rosemary, olive oil and 1.5L water.
Cover and bake at 150°C for 3-4 hours until very tender.
Stir in lemon juice and salt in the last 30 minutes.
Mash a few chickpeas to thicken and serve with extra olive oil.

recipes.title: Keftedes
recipes.description: Greek meatballs with mint, oregano and ouzo, fried until crisp.
steps: Soak bread in water, then squeeze dry.
Mix beef, pork, bread, grated onion, garlic, herbs, egg, ouzo and salt; knead well.
Rest 30 minutes in the fridge.
Shape into walnut-sized balls and roll lightly in flour.
Heat olive oil in a frying pan; fry meatballs in batches until deeply browned and cooked.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with lemon wedges.
